LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO GIVE PROPER NOTICE THAT HE IS CONTESTING THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF LARRY JACOBS' MOTION TO VACATE THE HEARING FOR DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTIONS TO VACATE ARE GRANTED. THE HEARING DATES OF SEPTEMBER 14 AND SEPTEMBER 20 FOR THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION ARE VACATED BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S NOTICES FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 437C. DEFENDANT'S REMEDY IS TO SEEK A TRIAL CONTINUANCE AND LEAVE TO CALENDAR THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WITHIN THE 30-DAY PERIOD PRIOR TO TRIAL IF NECESSARY. THE COURT SIGNED ONE ORDER FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on plaintiff Larry Jacobs' motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Jennifer Rodrigues, CSR 484.

Murlene J Randle, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Larry Jacobs.
Andrew M. Gshwind, Esq. appeared for defendant City & County of San Francisco.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and hearing argument from counsel the matter is SUBMITTED. Trial call in this matter is set for Monday, March 25, 2013. The Court shall issue an order on defendant's motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate The Hearing For Defendant'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Re Noticed For September 20, 2012; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-17-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Murlene J. Randle, Esq. and Robert Reed, Esq. of the L/O of Murlene Randle, Tel. 415.352.0189, for Plaintiff and moving party;
Andrew M. Gschwind, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3973, for Defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was discussed. Defendant failed to give proper notice that he is contesting the tentative ruling. The court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LARRY JACOBS' Motion To Vacate The Hearing For Defendant's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Re-Noticed For September 20, 2012 - The motions to vacate are granted. The hearing dates of September 14 and September 20 for the summary judgment motion are vacated because defendant's notices failed to comply with CCP 437c. Defendant's remedy is to seek a trial continuance and leave to calendar the motion for summary judgment within the 30-day period prior to trial if necessary. The Court signed one order for both motions. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
